reviews.review_msg: Students don't like to do work, and Paulo expects students to do work. This is why students don't like him. The work pays off, and if you're confused, contact him. He will meet you anywhere any time, and even phone you to make sure he makes himself clear. 6 novels isn't too much to read in a class.

Thinks that his is the only class you're taking all term: enough reading for 2 courses in 1. Participate in class or he'll be grumpy, everyone will suffer. Make sure that what you say won't prompt him to say something like, "Is what you're about to say going to be relevant?" Agree with his opinions!
This class was a book club.

David not only expresses genuine interest in his subject matter, but also in the learning experience and environment of his students. He is always available to help if you need it. Intersting, educational and fun. Excellent guest speakers and field trips.

David has a passion for his field, great sense of humour and never makes your ideas seem invalid.Lots of videos and a field trip or two. Is really there for his students. His partner Sophie McCall (Can Lit) is of equal calliber.

Dr. Kim was a caring professor, and her lectures were organized. However, her directions for essays were strict, and in a field such as English, they limit creativity and the writer's voice. Her feedback often left me unmotivated and made me question whether English was the right major for me.

prof is fair, encourages debate. 'Facilitator' type, but a little too passive, and seminars tend to lack focus. Student discussion is one thing, but for upper div courses, the prof needs to say more. It's a class, not a book club.
